item.title: Rigid Designation
item.description:
Kripke argues that some terms refer to the same object in every possible world where that object exists. Such terms are rigid designators.

Rigid designation separates a term’s reference from descriptive content that may vary across worlds.

item.title: Sense and Reference
item.description:
Frege distinguishes between sense (mode of presentation) and reference (the thing referred to). Sense provides the cognitive route by which a term picks out its referent.

This distinction helps explain how two expressions can refer to the same object while conveying different information.
